hero.thesis: I care how it's built, not just that it works.
hero.subtitle: Full Stack Developer building sustainable React, Node.js and TypeScript systems in production. Every claim here is backed by shipped work, not adjectives.

suites.case: ships to production — live wholesale platform
280+ tests written before any UI existed

about.title: Who you're reading
about.p1: I'm a Full Stack Developer with production experience. I currently work at ForIT, a software factory, where I design and implement features on a wholesale platform in production: query refactoring, a stock synchronization engine, and the migration of over 2,000 products. Test coverage is part of the process, not a final step.
about.p2: I apply TDD in my personal projects from the design phase. This means defining contracts before writing implementation, making separation of concerns explicit, and reducing debugging time in later iterations. TickPanic, my most substantial project, has 280+ tests covering entities, use cases, and services, all written before any UI existed.
about.p3: I'm looking for a role where architecture decisions are collaborative and grounded in explicit technical criteria. I want to work on systems with real constraints of scale or domain, where technical debt is managed rather than accumulated.

forit.context: Production wholesale platform in active use
forit.stack: React 18 + Vite, Node.js, Express, MySQL, Yarn Workspaces
bullets: Eliminated N+1 queries with direct performance impact on the branch management panel
Migrated 2,000+ products from legacy WordPress using internally developed XML/WXR scripts
Real-time inventory system: low-stock alerts, local replenishment tracking, and automatic deduction on bulk sales
Expanded test coverage with Vitest: providers, API and auth mocks, cryptographic functions, and controllers
forit.technical: Eliminating N+1 queries required data model analysis, blast radius assessment, and test validation before modifying production. The XML/WXR migration scripts were built from scratch with category mapping logic and duplicate handling, with no reference documentation for the source format.

projects.title: Projects
projects.intro: Three projects. Each is live, source-available, or both.

tickpanic.name: TickPanic
tickpanic.description: Real-time multiplayer application built with Clean Architecture, TDD, and server-side state synchronization via WebSockets.
tickpanic.stack: TypeScript 5, React 19, Fastify, Socket.io, PostgreSQL, Prisma ORM, Docker, pnpm workspaces, Nginx, Vitest

jardin.name: El Jardín de las Respuestas
jardin.description: Educational platform with a REST API: 13 endpoints, JWT authentication, role management, and a forum system. I worked on the API endpoints, the frontend integration, and UI improvements.
jardin.stack: NestJS, PostgreSQL, Prisma ORM, JWT, React

joblog.name: Job Log
joblog.description: Open source, serverless Chrome extension that logs job applications to Google Sheets using hybrid extraction via Gemini API.
joblog.stack: JavaScript, Chrome Extensions API (Manifest V3), Gemini API, Google Sheets API, OAuth

github.intro: Latest public commits pushed to my repositories. This section verifies itself, live.

skills.intro: Technologies listed by the context they were actually used in: production systems, personal projects with defined architecture, or external collaborations. This reflects real exposure, not a self-rating.
